questions.question:
Consider the following tasks:
  1. Superintendence, direction, and conduct of free and fair elections
  2. Preparation of electoral rolls for all elections to the Parliament, State Legislatures and the Office of the President and the Vice President
  3. Giving recognition to political parties and individuals contesting the election
  4. Proclamation of final verdict in the case of election disputes
Which of the above is the function of the Election Commission of India?
questions.solution:
The Election Commission of India (ECI) functions include:
  • 1. Superintendence: Overall control of elections.
  • 2. Rolls: Preparation of electoral rolls for Parliament and State legislatures.
  • 3. Recognition: Granting status to political parties.
  • Note: Election disputes (Statement 4) regarding MPs/MLAs are handled by the High Courts and the Supreme Court, not the ECI.

questions.question: Which one of the following statements correctly describes the Fourth Schedule of the Constitution of India?
questions.option_1: It contains the scheme of the distribution of powers between the Union and the States
questions.option_2: It contains the languages listed in the Constitution
questions.option_3: It contains the provisions regarding the administration of tribal areas
questions.option_4: It allocates seats in the Council of States
questions.solution: The Fourth Schedule of the Indian Constitution deals with the allocation of seats in the Rajya Sabha (Council of States) to the various States and Union Territories.

questions.question: Which one of the following Schedules of the Indian Constitution lists the names of States and specifies their territories?
questions.solution: The First Schedule lists the names of the States and Union Territories along with their territorial jurisdictions. Whenever a new state is created or a name is changed, this schedule must be amended.

questions.question: The Ninth Schedule to the Indian Constitution was added by
questions.solution: As discussed previously, the Ninth Schedule was the first addition to the Constitution, brought in by the First Amendment Act, 1951, to protect land reform laws from judicial review.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
  1. The joint sitting of the two houses of the Parliament in India is sanctioned under Article 108 of the Constitution
  2. The first joint sitting of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha was held in the year 1961
  3. The second joint sitting of the two Houses of the Indian Parliament was held to pass the Banking Service Commission (Repeal) Bill
Which of these statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
All three statements are historically and constitutionally correct:
  • Article 108: Provides for a Joint Sitting to resolve deadlocks on Ordinary Bills.
  • 1961: The first joint sitting was held for the Dowry Prohibition Bill.
  • Banking Service: The second was for the Banking Service Commission (Repeal) Bill in 1978.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution did the President give his assent to the ordinance on electoral reforms when it was sent back to him by the Union Cabinet without making any changes?
questions.solution: Article 123 grants the President the power to promulgate Ordinances during the recess of Parliament. These have the same force as an Act of Parliament.

questions.question: Which one of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution provides that 'It shall be the duty of the Union to protect every State against external aggression and internal disturbance'?
questions.solution: Article 355 imposes a duty on the Union to protect States against external aggression and internal disturbance. It also ensures that the government of every State is carried on in accordance with the provisions of the Constitution.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements: In India, stamp duties on financial transactions are:
  1. Levied and collected by the State Government
  2. Appropriated by the Union Government
Which of these statements is/are correct?
questions.solution: According to Article 268, stamp duties are levied by the Union but are collected and appropriated by the States. Therefore, both statements 1 and 2 in the question are technically framed as incorrect relative to the actual Article 268 mechanism.

questions.question: Which one of the following Bills must be passed by each House of the Indian Parliament separately, by special majority?
questions.solution:
A Constitution Amendment Bill (under Article 368) must be passed by each House separately by a special majority (majority of total membership and 2/3rd of members present and voting).
  • Unlike an Ordinary Bill, there is no provision for a Joint Sitting in case of a deadlock on an Amendment Bill.

questions.question:
With reference to Indian Public Finance, consider the following statements:
  1. Disbursements from Public Accounts of India are subject to the Vote of Parliament
  2. The Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of a Consolidated Fund, a Public Account, and a Contingency Fund for each State
  3. Appropriations and disbursements under the Railway Budget are subject to the same form of Parliamentary control as other appropriations and disbursements
Which of these statements given above are correct?
questions.solution:
Statement 2 and 3 are correct:
  • Public Accounts: Unlike the Consolidated Fund, payments from the Public Account do not require parliamentary vote (they are mostly banking transactions). Thus Statement 1 is incorrect.
  • Funds: Every state has its own Consolidated Fund, Public Account, and Contingency Fund.
  • Railways: Since the merger of the Railway Budget with the Union Budget in 2017, they follow the same parliamentary procedures.

questions.question: Which one of the following rights was described by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar as the heart and soul of the Constitution?
questions.solution:
Dr. B.R. Ambedkar called Article 32 (Right to Constitutional Remedies) the 'Heart and Soul' of the Constitution.
  • He argued that without the power to enforce rights through the Supreme Court, all other rights are meaningless.

questions.question: Which one of the following Articles of the Directive Principles of State Policy deals with the promotion of international peace and security?
questions.solution:
As per Article 51 (DPSP), the State is directed to promote international peace and security and encourage the settlement of international disputes by arbitration.
  • 48A: Environment. 43A: Workers' participation. 41: Right to work.

questions.question: Which one of the following amendments to the Indian Constitution empowers the President to send back any matter for reconsideration by the Council of Ministers?
questions.solution:
The 44th Amendment Act (1978) restored the balance of power by allowing the President to send back the advice of the Council of Ministers for reconsideration once.
  • However, if the Council sends the same advice again, the President must accept it.

questions.question: In the Indian Constitution, the Right to Equality is granted by five Articles. They are:
questions.solution:
The Right to Equality is covered under Articles 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18.
  • These cover Equality before law, Prohibition of discrimination, Equality of opportunity in employment, Abolition of untouchability, and Abolition of titles.

questions.question: The purpose of the inclusion of Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution is to establish
questions.solution:
The Constitution explicitly states (Article 38) that the Directive Principles are aimed at establishing a social and economic democracy.
  • Political democracy is primarily ensured through Fundamental Rights and Universal Adult Suffrage.

questions.question: The 93rd Constitutional Amendment Bill deals with
questions.option_1: Continuation of reservation for backward classes in government employment
questions.option_2: Free and compulsory education for all children between the age of 6 and 14 years
questions.option_3: Reservation of 30 percent posts for women in government recruitments
questions.option_4: Allocation of more number of parliamentary seats for recently created States
questions.solution:
The 93rd Amendment Bill (which became the 86th Amendment Act) provided for Free and Compulsory Education for children aged 6–14.
  • It inserted Article 21A as a Fundamental Right.

questions.question: If a new state of the Indian Union is to be created, which one of the following schedules of the Constitution must be amended?
questions.solution: The First Schedule must be amended to add the name and territory of a new state. The Fourth Schedule would also likely be amended to allocate Rajya Sabha seats to the new state.

questions.question: Which one of the following statements correctly describes the Fourth Schedule of the Constitution of India?
questions.option_1: It lists the distribution of powers between the Union and the states
questions.option_2: It contains the languages listed in the Constitution
questions.option_3: It contains the provisions regarding the administration of tribal areas
questions.option_4: It allocates seats in the Council of States
questions.solution: The Fourth Schedule is dedicated to the allocation of seats in the Council of States (Rajya Sabha) to the states and Union Territories.

questions.question: The Parliament can make any law for the whole or any part of India for implementing International treaties
questions.option_1: With the consent of all the States
questions.option_2: With the consent of the majority of States
questions.option_3: With the consent of the States concerned
questions.option_4: Without the consent of any State
questions.solution: Under Article 253, the Parliament has the power to make any law for the whole or any part of India to implement international treaties or agreements, and it can do so without the consent of any state.

questions.question: A British citizen staying in India cannot claim Right to
questions.option_3: Protection of life and personal liberty
questions.solution:
Fundamental Rights like Article 19 (Freedom of trade, profession, movement, etc.) are available only to citizens of India.
  • Foreigners (like a British citizen) can claim Article 14 (Equality) and Article 21 (Life), but not the specific freedoms under Article 19.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements: An amendment to the Constitution of India can be initiated by the
  1. Lok Sabha
  2. Rajya Sabha
  3. State Legislatures
  4. President
Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
A Constitution Amendment Bill can only be initiated in either House of Parliament (Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha).
  • It cannot be initiated in State Legislatures or by the President himself.

questions.question: The Constitution of India recognizes
questions.solution: The Indian Constitution (specifically Articles 29 and 30) recognizes minorities based on religion and language. It does not explicitly use the term 'ethnic' or 'racial' minorities.

questions.question: Which one of the following schedules of the Constitution of India contains provisions regarding the anti-defection Act?
questions.solution: The Tenth Schedule, containing Anti-Defection provisions, was added to the Constitution in 1985 to prevent legislators from changing parties for personal gain after being elected.
